mapping_title: Agent tool permission treated as action authority
domain: Agent governance and autonomous workflow control

native_domain_object.description: A record showing that an agent, workflow, or automation had access to a tool, API, connector, or execution capability under defined conditions.

source_claim.statement: The agent or workflow had the recorded tool access permission under the specified identity, scope, and configuration.
source_claim.scope: Tool access permission, configured identity, tool scope, time, and declared operational context only.

evidence_preserved.description: The tool permission record is preserved as evidence that the agent or workflow had access to the tool.
The agent or workflow identity reference is preserved as evidence of the subject associated with the permission.

boundary_crossing.transition_description: A tool access permission is consumed by a downstream workflow as if it authorizes a consequential action using that tool.

downstream_assumption.statement: The existence of tool permission is treated as if it establishes authority to perform a consequential action.

unresolved_questions: What actor or institution has authority to approve the consequential action?
What policy, mandate, or delegated authority basis governs the action?
What additional evidence would bind the action to an authorized purpose, scope, and accountable actor?
non_claims: This mapping does not establish action authority.
This mapping does not establish runtime authorization.
This mapping does not establish legal sufficiency.
This mapping does not establish policy compliance.
This mapping does not convert tool access into permission to act.
